name: Insider Threat — After-Hours Records Snooping
description: Night-shift nurse accesses VIP patient records, celebrity records, and coworker records outside their department. Low and slow — no network anomalies, only detectable via Epic SIEM patterns. This is a pure Epic audit scenario.

events.description: Break-the-glass on VIP patient — not assigned
Viewing coworker records — mental health, pregnancy
Second break-the-glass in one shift — highly suspicious
Extensive chart review of celebrity records

network_correlation.description: No network anomalies — this is purely an Epic audit/behavioral detection scenario. Network traffic looks completely normal. This is the point: not all threats have network indicators.

indicators: Multiple break-the-glass events in a single shift (normally 0-1 per month for a nurse)
Accessing patients in EXECUTIVE HEALTH department — far outside MED-SURG 3W scope
Name-based patient searches for coworkers during off-hours
Rapid context switching between unrelated patients (not clinical workflow pattern)
Export/print of records for non-assigned patients
All activity from a single workstation IP during 11PM-3AM shift
